title: portfolioacty
text:
Hello! My name is Luc and I am a **Master’s student in Statistics & Risk Economics** at Paris Nanterre University, specializing in Data Science and Actuarial track.
Passionate about making data speak, this website showcases my academic and personal projects in statistics, econometrics, and data science.
📬 **Looking for a 1 or 2-year apprenticeship starting September 2026.**
Feel free to explore my [Projects](projects.qmd) page or check out my [GitHub Profile](https://github.com/luc-zhan).

title: projects.qmd
text:
## Project 1: Credit Risk Modeling & Financial Econometrics

📌 Project Overview
The goal of this project was to predict default probabilities for bank clients using credit data. I implemented statistical models to evaluate and mitigate financial risk.
**Keywords:** Logistic Regression, Risk Analysis, R, ggplot2, Data Cleaning.
**Dataset:** 5,000+ observations with financial indicators (income, debt ratio, credit history).
### 📊 Exploratory Data Analysis & Visualization Before modeling, I analyzed the distribution of our target variable against key risk indicators.

# 1. Charger les packages nécessaires
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)

# 2. Simulation d'un jeu de données de risques (remplace par tes vraies données !)
set.seed(42)
data_risk <- data.frame(
  Debt_Ratio = c(rnorm(200, mean = 0.3, sd = 0.1), rnorm(100, mean = 0.6, sd = 0.15)),
  Default = as.factor(c(rep("No", 200), rep("Yes", 100)))
)

# 3. Génération d'un graphique pro avec ggplot2
ggplot(data_risk, aes(x = Default, y = Debt_Ratio, fill = Default)) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.7) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("#2A9D8F", "#E76F51")) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Debt Ratio Distribution by Default Status",
    x = "Client Defaulted?",
    y = "Debt-to-Income Ratio",
    fill = "Status"
  )








### 📈 Statistical Modeling I fitted a logistic regression model to estimate the impact of the debt ratio on the probability of default:

# Ajustement du modèle logistique
model <- glm(Default ~ Debt_Ratio, data = data_risk, family = binomial)

# Affichage des coefficients du modèle
summary(model)$coefficients

             Estimate Std. Error   z value     Pr(>|z|)
(Intercept) -9.742214   1.159550 -8.401716 4.400011e-17
Debt_Ratio  21.325933   2.669639  7.988322 1.367882e-15


### 🎯 Key Takeaways **Insight:** Every 10% increase in the debt-to-income ratio multiplies the odds of defaulting by $1.5$. **Application:** This model helps automate the first layer of credit approval in retail banking.
